title: Redesign DashboardPage calendar with stylish, elegant spiritual aesthetic

requirements.text: Redesign the calendar view in DashboardPage with a visually attractive, elegant, and spiritual style consistent with the app's terracotta/cream theme. The calendar must feature: a refined month header with stylish prev/next navigation arrows, day-of-week labels styled in terracotta accent color, date cells with generous rounded corners and subtle hover/focus states, clear visual distinction for today's date (e.g., terracotta border or highlight ring), a bold filled terracotta background for the selected/active date cell with cream text, dots or subtle indicators on dates that have jap data recorded, smooth transitions on interaction, and consistent font sizing using the app's existing Cinzel/Lato font stack. The overall calendar container should use a card-style surface (elevated with a soft shadow) with a dark-brown or cream background depending on the current theme mode. All existing functionality (date selection, jap count display, timezone-safe date keys) must be fully preserved.

quotes: still calender style to view not proper make it stylisy calendar

acceptanceCriteria: The calendar renders inside a visually elevated card with rounded corners and a soft shadow, using the terracotta/cream/dark-brown palette.
Month/year header is clearly displayed with styled prev/next navigation controls.
Day-of-week labels (Sun–Sat) are styled in the terracotta accent color.
Today's date is visually distinguished from other dates (e.g., terracotta ring or border).
The selected date cell has a solid terracotta background with cream-colored text.
Dates with jap data recorded display a small dot or accent indicator beneath the date number.
Hover and focus states on date cells are smooth and theme-consistent.
The calendar uses Cinzel or Lato fonts consistent with the rest of the app.
All existing date-click functionality (showing jap count for selected date) continues to work correctly.
The calendar is fully visible and non-overflowing on both mobile and desktop viewports.
Both light and dark theme modes apply appropriate background and text colors to the calendar.

constraints: Do not alter the date key storage format or timezone-safe logic already implemented for reading/writing jap counts.
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui.
Preserve all existing calendar functionality: date selection, jap count retrieval from localStorage, and selected-date display.
nonGoals: No changes to any other page beyond DashboardPage.
No changes to the chart/graph sections of DashboardPage.
No new calendar library integrations — style the existing custom calendar implementation.
